perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, the Licensor, of which New Mountain Finance Advisers, BDC, L.L.C., a Delaware limited liability company (the "Investment Advisor") is an affiliate, is the owner of all right, title, and interest in and to the mark "NMF" (the "Licensed Mark") in the United States of America, Canada and the European Union (the "Territory") in connection with "financial services, namely, investment advisory and investment management services for pooled investment vehicles, private investment funds, and investment accounts; investment management services for others; private equity services, namely, providing expansion and growth capital in the form of private equity investments; financial services, namely, private equity and public equity capital investment; private and public equity investment management services; providing private equity fund investments; investment services, namely, asset acquisition, consultation, development, research and management services; capital investment services; equity capital investment; financial services, namely, debt and equity investment services in private companies, namely, investment to support business expansions, acquisitions, management buyouts and recapitalizations" (the "Licensed Services"), and Licensor has been and is currently using, either on its own or through its related companies or licensees (such as, but not limited to, the Investment Advisor) the Licensed Mark;
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, the Licensor, of which New Mountain Finance Advisers, BDC, L.L.C., a Delaware limited liability company (the "Investment Advisor") is an affiliate, is the owner of all right, title, and interest in and to the mark "NMF" (the "Licensed Mark") in connection with "financial services, namely, investment advisory and investment management services for pooled investment vehicles, private investment funds, and investment accounts; investment management services for others; private equity services, namely, providing expansion and growth capital in the form of private equity investments; financial services, namely, private equity and public equity capital investment; private and public equity investment management services; providing private equity fund investments; investment services, namely, asset acquisition, consultation, development, research and management services; capital investment services; equity capital investment; financial services, namely, debt and equity investment services in private companies, namely, investment to support business expansions, acquisitions, management buyouts and recapitalizations" (the "Licensed Services"), and Licensor has been and is currently using the Licensed Mark;
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "either on its own or through its related companies or licensees (such as, but not limited to, the Investment Advisor)", we create an in-text contradiction. Later sections, such as 5.1, state that the Investment Advisor is a third-party beneficiary. However, removing the explicitly stated use of the Licensed Mark by related companies raises questions about whether the Investment Advisor's use of the mark is still covered under the agreement, creating uncertainty around the third-party beneficiary status.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1. Third Party Beneficiaries. The parties agree that the Investment Advisor shall be a third party beneficiary of this Agreement, and shall have the rights and protections provided to the Licensee under this Agreement. Nothing in this Agreement, either express or implied, is intended to or shall confer upon any third party other than the Investment Advisor any legal or equitable right, benefit or remedy of any nature whatsoever under or by reason of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the Licensor is using the Licensed Mark 'either on its own or through its related companies or licensees (such as, but not limited to, the Investment Advisor)'. The changed text omits this phrase, which creates a contradiction with section 5.1 that designates the Investment Advisor as a third-party beneficiary of the agreement and grants it the rights and protections provided to the Licensee. By removing the explicit statement about related companies and licensees using the mark, the changed text introduces ambiguity as to whether the Investment Advisor's use of the mark is still covered under the agreement. This casts doubt on the Investment Advisor's status as a third-party beneficiary, causing an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1.1. License. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, the Licensor hereby grants to the Licensee, and the Licensee hereby accepts from the Licensor, a personal, non-exclusive, royalty-free right and license to use the Licensed Mark in the Territory solely and exclusively as a component of the Licensee's own company name and in connection with the Licensed Services and any business provided in conjunction therewith by such Licensee. During the term of this Agreement, the Licensee shall use the Licensed Mark only to the extent permitted under this Agreement, and except as provided above, neither the Licensee nor any of its affiliates, owners, directors, officers, employees or agents shall otherwise use the Licensed Mark or any derivatives without the prior express written consent of the Licensor in its sole and absolute discretion. All rights not expressly granted to the Licensee hereunder shall remain the exclusive property of the Licensor. Upon written notification by the Licensor Source: NMF SLF I, INC., 10-12G/A, 1/15/2020 to the Licensee of noncompliance with the Licensor's quality standards in any material respect, such Licensee shall take appropriate steps, in a commercially reasonable time frame, not to exceed sixty (60) days, to cure such noncompliance.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1. License. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, the Licensor hereby grants to the Licensee, and the Licensee hereby accepts from the Licensor, a personal, non-exclusive, royalty-free right and license to use the Licensed Mark in the Territory solely and exclusively as a component of the Licensee's own company name and in connection with any business. During the term of this Agreement, the Licensee shall use the Licensed Mark only to the extent permitted under this Agreement, and except as provided above, neither the Licensee nor any of its affiliates, owners, directors, officers, employees or agents shall otherwise use the Licensed Mark or any derivatives without the prior express written consent of the Licensor in its sole and absolute discretion. All rights not expressly granted to the Licensee hereunder shall remain the exclusive property of the Licensor. Upon written notification by the Licensor Source: NMF SLF I, INC., 10-12G/A, 1/15/2020 to the Licensee of noncompliance with the Licensor's quality standards in any material respect, such Licensee shall take appropriate steps, in a commercially reasonable time frame, not to exceed sixty (60) days, to cure such noncompliance.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase "the Licensed Services" was removed from section 1.1, causing contradiction with the recital. It has been established that the mark "NMF" is in connection to a pre-agreed set of financial services, without that limitation, the Licensee can use the mark with any business and not only with the services established previously. This creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, the Licensor, of which New Mountain Finance Advisers, BDC, L.L.C., a Delaware limited liability company (the "Investment Advisor") is an affiliate, is the owner of all right, title, and interest in and to the mark "NMF" (the "Licensed Mark") in the United States of America, Canada and the European Union (the "Territory") in connection with "financial services, namely, investment advisory and investment management services for pooled investment vehicles, private investment funds, and investment accounts; investment management services for others; private equity services, namely, providing expansion and growth capital in the form of private equity investments; financial services, namely, private equity and public equity capital investment; private and public equity investment management services; providing private equity fund investments; investment services, namely, asset acquisition, consultation, development, research and management services; capital investment services; equity capital investment; financial services, namely, debt and equity investment services in private companies, namely, investment to support business expansions, acquisitions, management buyouts and recapitalizations" (the "Licensed Services"), and Licensor has been and is currently using, either on its own or through its related companies or licensees (such as, but not limited to, the Investment Advisor) the Licensed Mark;
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 1.1 limited the use of the Licensed Mark to "the Licensed Services," which are specifically defined as "financial services" in the 'WHEREAS' clause. The changed text removes this limitation, allowing the Licensee to use the mark in connection with any business. This directly contradicts the 'WHEREAS' clause, as the license is no longer restricted to the pre-agreed financial services, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4.2. Effect of Termination. Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement, all rights granted to the Licensee under this Agreement with respect to the Licensed Mark shall cease, and the Licensee shall immediately delete the term "NMF" from its corporate name and shall discontinue all other use of the Licensed Mark. For twenty-four (24) months following termination of this Agreement, the Licensee shall specify on all public- facing materials in a prominent place and in prominent typeface that the Licensee is no longer operating under the Licensed Mark, is no longer associated with the Licensor, or such other notice as may be deemed necessary by the Licensor in its sole discretion in its prosecution, defense, and/or settlement of any Third Party Claim.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2. Effect of Termination. Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement, all rights granted to the Licensee under this Agreement with respect to the Licensed Mark shall cease, and the Licensee shall immediately delete the term "NMF" from its corporate name and shall discontinue all other use of the Licensed Mark. For twenty-four (24) months following termination of this Agreement, the Licensee shall specify on all public- facing materials that the Licensee is no longer operating under the Licensed Mark, is no longer associated with the Licensor.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the phrase "or such other notice as may be deemed necessary by the Licensor in its sole discretion in its prosecution, defense, and/or settlement of any Third Party Claim", we remove the Licensor's ability to dictate specific messaging post-termination related to Third Party Claims. Section 2.3 grants the Licensor the exclusive right to prosecute, defend, and/or settle claims related to the Licensed Mark. Therefore, the inability to dictate necessary notice post-termination directly contradicts their right to protect the Licensed Mark.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.3. Notification of Infringement. Each party shall immediately notify the other party and provide to the other party all relevant background facts upon becoming aware of (a) any registrations of, or applications for registration of, marks in the Territory that do or may conflict with the Licensor's rights in the Licensed Mark or the rights granted to the Licensee under this Agreement, (b) any infringements or misuses of the Licensed Mark in the Territory by any third party ("Third Party Infringement") or (c) any claim that Licensee's use of the Licensed Mark infringes the intellectual property rights of any third party in the Territory ("Third Party Claim"). The Licensor shall have the exclusive right, but not the obligation, to prosecute, defend and/or settle in its sole discretion, all actions, proceedings and claims involving any Third Party Infringement or Third Party Claim, and to take any other action that it deems necessary or proper for the protection and preservation of its rights in the Licensed Mark. The Licensee shall cooperate with the Licensor in the prosecution, defense or settlement of such actions, proceedings or claims.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed the Licensor discretion in determining necessary notices related to Third Party Claims. The changed text removes this discretion. Section 2.3 grants the Licensor the exclusive right to defend and settle claims. Removing the ability to dictate necessary notice post-termination directly impairs the Licensor's ability to protect the Licensed Mark, therefore creating a direct conflict between the modified Section 4.2 and Section 2.3.
